task_completion.description: Access the raw film dataset containing the mixed duration formats.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet, document, or data viewer is open showing the list of 12 films with their raw, uncleaned duration formats (e.g., '45', '1:35', 'about 95 minutes', '2h 15m').

task_completion.description: Clean and standardize the film duration data into a uniform minute format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column or list showing the converted durations in pure minutes (e.g., converting '2h 15m' to 135, '1:35' to 95).

task_completion.description: Calculate the total time required for each film by applying the time constraints (Q&A and transition buffers).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Calculations or a dedicated column showing the base duration plus a 20-minute buffer (15 min Q&A + 5 min transition) for the films.

task_completion.description: Assign the films to the available festival venues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draft or final schedule explicitly maps films to either the 'Main Theater' or 'Gallery Space'.

task_completion.description: Generate the final viable screening schedule using the valid time slots.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A completed schedule document or spreadsheet showing films assigned to the specific valid time slots (2:00 PM, 4:00 PM, 6:30 PM, 8:30 PM) without overlapping conflicts in the same venue.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate GUI applications (like a spreadsheet or text editor) to clean data and build the schedule.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet or document editor interface, rather than just outputting a final schedule via a single terminal script.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual provided synthetic dataset without hallucinating data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The film titles in the final schedule match the 12 films from the original dataset, with no fabricated titles or altered venue names.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the data cleaning and scheduling process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps, formulas, or manual edits are visible in the screenshots, proving the agent didn't just copy-paste a pre-computed solution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset contains exactly 12 films. Durations must be converted to pure minutes (e.g., '2h 15m' = 135 min, '1:35' = 95 min). Total scheduled time per film must include a 20-minute buffer (15 min Q&A + 5 min transition). Valid venues are Main Theater (capacity 150) and Gallery Space (capacity 50). Valid time slots are 2:00 PM, 4:00 PM, 6:30 PM, and 8:30 PM.
